method: Parse DOMEX MERGED lanes S363-S373 from SWARM-LANES.md + archive. Cross-tabulate by outcome label and sub-era (pre/post cooldown at S370). Compare L/lane and dispatch share.
key_findings: MIXED dispatch share rose 62.9% -> 80.0% (+17.1pp) — MIXED_BONUS steering confirmed
MIXED L/lane maintained at 1.40 (vs 1.36 pre-S363, 1.42 at S363) — no degradation
Meta concentration: cooldown crushed 31.2% -> 11.1% — L-676 fix effective
Domain diversity per lane improved post-cooldown (0.89 vs 0.69 domains/lane)
STRUGGLING domains zero-dispatched (0/25) — effectively frozen out by scoring
PROVEN domains under-dispatched (12%) — nk-complexity only domain maintaining presence
Abbreviation map coverage still incomplete — 52/122 pre-S363 lanes have UNKNOWN labels
unexpected: The MIXED_BONUS and cooldown are COMPLEMENTARY mechanisms that solve different problems (MIXED_BONUS: what to prioritize; cooldown: what to rotate away from). Neither alone is sufficient. Also: meta spiked to 31% in S363-S369 BETWEEN the MIXED_BONUS addition and cooldown implementation — the temporal gap between scoring fixes creates concentration windows.
prescriptions: Keep MIXED_BONUS at +2.0 — yield maintained, dispatch steering confirmed
Add STRUGGLING floor: minimum 5% dispatch share to prevent domain starvation
Full measurement at S383 (20 sessions post-S363) for statistical power
Continue cooldown at current parameters — meta suppression working
confidence: Measured (n=25 lanes S363-S373, n=147 total, 2 sub-eras analyzed)
